events.name: New York Giants at Green Bay Packers

headlines.description: — Daniel Jones and Saquon Barkley spoiled the Green Bay Packers' international debut.
headlines.shortLinkText: Giants spoil Packers international debut with 27-22 win

headlines.description: — Josh Allen made a mental note after seeing receiver Gabe Davis have a bounce in his step a day earlier during the Buffalo Bills' final walk-through session before facing the Steelers.
headlines.shortLinkText: Allen picks apart Steelers secondary in Bills' 38-3 win

events.name: Los Angeles Chargers at Cleveland Browns

headlines.description: — Brandon Staley trusted his gut, the analytics and his offense.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chargers hang on, beat Browns 30-28 after LA coach's gamble

headlines.description: — Kirk Cousins and the Minnesota Vikings established a remarkable rhythm early. Their resiliency surfaced again late.
headlines.shortLinkText: Cousins, Vikings snap to life with late TD, beat Bears 29-22

events.name: Detroit Lions at New England Patriots

headlines.description: — Nostalgia was draped over the New England Patriots on Sunday, from the team's throwback, 1980s-era jerseys to the minuteman logo painted at midfield.
headlines.shortLinkText: Stevenson, Patriots' D back Zappe in 29-0 win over Lions

events.name: Seattle Seahawks at New Orleans Saints

headlines.description: — Don't ask New Orleans Saints tight end Taysom Hill what position he really plays.
headlines.shortLinkText: Hill accounts for 4 TDs, Saints top Seahawks 39-32

events.name: Miami Dolphins at New York Jets

headlines.description: — The New York Jets got a big play on defense and then kept running on offense.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jets run past Dolphins 40-17, snap 12-game skid vs. AFC East

events.name: Atlanta Falcons at Tampa Bay Buccaneers

headlines.description: — Tom Brady has never lost to the Atlanta Falcons. He hasn't lost three straight games in over two decades. Those streaks stayed intact Sunday with timely assistance from a disputed roughing-the-passer penalty.
headlines.shortLinkText: Tom Brady throws for 351 yards, Bucs beat Falcons 21-15

headlines.description: — A teammate yelled at David Long Jr. from a corner of the locker room, “Keep that game ball!”
headlines.shortLinkText: Titans get late pick to beat Commanders, win 3rd in a row

headlines.description: — Dameon Pierce initially got hit behind the line of scrimmage. He slipped Rayshawn Jenkins' tackle and then six or seven more on his way to a...
headlines.shortLinkText: Pierce's run, Walker's gaffe help Texans topple Jags again

headlines.description: — After failing to land Deshaun Watson in the offseason, the Carolina Panthers had a choice of whether to pursue Jimmy Garoppolo or Baker Mayfield in an effort to upgrade at quarterback.
headlines.shortLinkText: Garoppolo, stingy defense lead 49ers past Panthers 37-15

events.name: Dallas Cowboys at Los Angeles Rams

headlines.description: — On the Rams' third offensive play, Dallas' Dorance Armstrong bashed through their offensive line and hit Matthew Stafford. DeMarcus Lawrence scooped up the quarterback's fumble and rumbled into the end zone.
headlines.shortLinkText: Dominant D puts Cowboys past Rams 22-10 for 4th straight win

headlines.description: — The Philadelphia Eagles are still the NFL's only undefeated team, winning their fifth game in a row thanks to a whole lot of grit and a bit of good fortune.
headlines.shortLinkText: Eagles stay undefeated, hang on to beat Cardinals 20-17

headlines.description: — The Baltimore Ravens essentially have two versions of the victory formation.
headlines.shortLinkText: Tucker's leg lifts Ravens to 19-17 victory over Bengals
